forecast.name: Tonight
forecast.detailedForecast: Mostly clear, with a low around 38. West wind around 6 mph.

weather_report:
In Boston this afternoon, the skies wear a weighty veil of clouds, gently dimming the light into a soft, muted brightness that imbues the day with a tranquil, reflective quality. The high temperature hovers near a crisp fifty-six, with a delicate west wind whispering through the streets at a steady seven miles per hour—a reminder of the unpredictable warmth that will soon beckon with the promise of spring. As evening unfolds, the clouds will ease, parting like curtains to reveal a vast, star-speckled sky while temperatures ease down toward the thirty-eighth mark. 

For those venturing out, a light jacket layered over a sweater will be both practical and comfortable; the lingering chill urges a thoughtful embrace of warmth. This gentle weather may inspire a stroll along the Charles, where the path seems to welcome quiet contemplation and the simple pleasures of nature. With the day winding down, consider settling into one of the cozy cafés for a warming cup of tea, perhaps indulging in a good book as the world drifts toward nightfall in a serene embrace.
